title: habrothamnus aurantiacus
intro: Habrothamnus aurantiacus, also known as the orange mikan or orange California buckwheat, is a plant species native to the southwestern United States and northern Mexico. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its various medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial effects. The health benefits of Habrothamnus aurantiacus include reducing inflammation and improving cardiovascular health, as well as its potential to aid in the treatment of certain cancers and other diseases. The plant can be prepared in various herbal forms, including teas, infusions, and capsules, making it a popular choice among herbalists and natural health practitioners.
